cards.front: What is a package in Java?
cards.back: A package in Java is a way to organize related classes, interfaces, and other types into a single unit.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the import statement in Java?
cards.back: The import statement in Java is used to import classes, interfaces, and other types from other packages.

cards.front: What is the difference between public and default (package) access in Java?
cards.back: In Java, public access is visible to all classes, while default (package) access is visible only within the package.

cards.front: What is the 'this' keyword in Java?
cards.back: In Java, 'this' refers to the current object, allowing it to access its own members and take part in method overriding and method overloading.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the 'super' keyword in Java?
cards.back: In Java, 'super' is used to access members of a superclass and to call superclass methods in a subclass.
